title: Pi: persistent Focus State widget via setWidget
description: ctx.ui.setWidget below editor showing: frame title, WBM status, decision/constraint/failure counts. Always visible. Updated on each turn_end. Per doc 44 §37.3

title: Pi: register Focusa as Pi model provider
description: Register Focusa as selectable Pi model provider. FILE: focusa-pi-bridge.ts. pi.registerProvider('focusa', {baseUrl:'http://127.0.0.1:8787/proxy/v1', apiKey:'FOCUSA_AUTH_TOKEN', api:'openai-completions', models:[{id:'kimi-via-focusa', name:'Kimi K2.5 (via Focusa)', contextWindow:262144, maxTokens:8192, ...}]}). User /model to switch. VERIFY: /model shows focusa/kimi-via-focusa option. REF: doc 44 §33.6

title: Pi: /wbm command — inbound context injection
description: Implement /wbm command — inbound context injection.  FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts (inside skeleton from focusa-e1w)  COMMAND: /wbm on|off|status|deep|flush|decisions|ships  ON before_agent_start (when wbmEnabled):   1. Fetch Context Core: GET http://127.0.0.1:7400/me (2s timeout)      Extract: state.mode, state.interruptibility, timely.local_time, timely.circadian_phase   2. Read objectives: fs.readFileSync('/data/wirebot/objectives.yaml', 'utf-8')      Extract P1 asset name + current_milestone   3. Fetch Scoreboard: GET http://127.0.0.1:8100/v1/score with Bearer $SCOREBOARD_TOKEN (2s timeout)      Extract: drift.score, drift.signal, season.record, streak.current   4. Fetch Focusa: GET http://127.0.0.1:8787/v1/focus/stack (2s timeout)      Extract: active frame title + goal   5. Read SOUL.md pillars: fs.readFileSync('/home/wirebot/clawd/SOUL.md', 'utf-8').slice(0, 500)   6. Fetch wiki decisions: shell exec 'wb wiki search tag:decision --format json --limit 3'    Assemble ~500 token context string and return:     { systemPrompt: event.systemPrompt + '\n\n' + wbmContext }  SCOREBOARD_TOKEN: process.env.SCOREBOARD_TOKEN or read from /run/wirebot/scoreboard.env  FALLBACK: Each fetch individually wrapped in try/catch with 2s timeout.   Failure = 'Source: unavailable' line, not a crash.  VERIFY: /wbm on → next prompt shows [WIREBOT MODE ACTIVE] block. /wbm off → block disappears. DEPENDS: focusa-e1w (skeleton must exist) REF: doc 44 §29, UNIFIED_ORGANISM_SPEC §9.9

title: Build /wbm Pi extension — Wirebot Mode with two-way bridge
description: Master bead for /wbm Pi extension. Umbrella — sub-beads cover each component.  BUILD ORDER:   1. focusa-e1w: extension skeleton (commands, hooks registered)   2. focusa-ygb: Pi HarnessType enum in Rust   3. focusa-trfj: auto-push Focus Frame on session start   4. focusa-bvet: behavioral instructions in system prompt   5. focusa-yw9d: focusa_decide/constraint/failure tools   6. focusa-zedk: injection layering rules   7. focusa-6zz: session scoping enforcement   8. focusa-wzk: /wbm inbound context injection   9. focusa-6dm: /wbm outbound work meta extraction   10. focusa-at0z: turn-level tracking (start/append/complete)   11. focusa-qt94: Focusa-owned compaction   12. focusa-6x7h: state persistence via appendEntry   13. focusa-4v3: outage passthrough + auto-reconnect  ALL FILES in: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge/ (directory with package.json for deps) ENTRY POINT: src/index.ts VERIFY: Full end-to-end test per §35.3 user workflow. REF: UNIFIED_ORGANISM_SPEC §9.9, doc 44 §29

title: Pi: clean session close via session_shutdown
description: Clean session close on shutdown. FILE: focusa-pi-bridge.ts. HOOK: session_shutdown. 1. flushWorkMeta() 2. persistFocusaState() 3. POST :8787/v1/session/close 4. POST /v1/instances/disconnect 5. pi.exec wb me --set pi_active=false. VERIFY: Ctrl+C closes Focusa session. REF: doc 44 §33.8 §37.9

title: Pi: use pi.exec for wb CLI instead of fetch
description: Replace fetch() calls to scoreboard/wiki with pi.exec('wb', [...args]). wb handles auth, config, retries. More reliable. Works if ports change. Per doc 44 §33.9

title: Pi: Focusa session resume on Pi /resume (not new session)
description: Resume Focusa session on Pi /resume instead of starting new.  FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts HOOK: session_start  IMPLEMENTATION:   1. Scan ctx.sessionManager.getEntries() for custom entries with customType='focusa-wbm-state'   2. If found: extract saved focusaSessionId   3. GET http://127.0.0.1:8787/v1/status (2s timeout)   4. If status.session.session_id === savedSessionId → reconnect (don't start new)   5. If different or error → start new session, update appendEntry with new ID   6. Restore wbmEnabled, cataloguedDecisions, cataloguedFacts from saved state  VERIFY: Start Pi session with /wbm on. Exit. /resume same session. WBM auto-restores. Focusa shows SAME session_id. DEPENDS: focusa-6x7h (appendEntry persistence must exist) REF: doc 44 §36.4

title: Pipeline fix: build Focusa event memory extractor
description: Build Focusa event memory extractor.  NO CODE EXISTS for extracting memories from Focusa events. Build from scratch.  CREATE: /data/wirebot/bin/focusa-event-extract.sh (or Go endpoint)  LOGIC:   1. Connect to Focusa SQLite: /home/wirebot/focusa/~/.focusa/focusa.sqlite   2. Query events not yet extracted:      SELECT id, payload_json FROM events       WHERE json_extract(payload_json, '$.type') IN ('TurnCompleted','FocusStateUpdated')      AND id > $WATERMARK      ORDER BY timestamp ASC LIMIT 20   3. For each TurnCompleted: extract assistant_output from payload      POST :8100/v1/memory/extract-conversation {user_message: raw_user_input, assistant_message: assistant_output, surface: 'focusa'}   4. For each FocusStateUpdated: extract decisions/constraints/failures from delta      Directly queue as memories: POST :8100/v1/memory/queue {memory_text: 'Decision: X', source_type: 'focusa_event', ...}   5. Update watermark file: /data/wirebot/scoreboard/focusa_event_watermark.txt  SCHEDULE: Add to nightly hygiene or separate timer RATE: 20 events per run, 2s between each VERIFY: After run, wb memory list shows new pending items sourced from focusa_event. REF: UNIFIED_ORGANISM_SPEC §10A.6 gap 3

title: Pi: pass token counts from Pi usage to Focusa turn/complete
description: Pass Pi token counts to Focusa. FILE: focusa-pi-bridge.ts. HOOK: turn_end. If event.message.role=assistant and event.message.usage: include usage.input as prompt_tokens and usage.output as completion_tokens in POST :8787/v1/turn/complete. VERIFY: curl :8787/v1/telemetry/tokens shows non-null. REF: doc 44 §35.5

title: Pi: fix port references 4777 to 8787 in doc 44
description: Fix port references from 4777 to 8787 in doc 44.  FILE: /home/wirebot/focusa/docs/44-pi-focusa-integration-spec.md  SEARCH: 4777 REPLACE: 8787  Specific locations:   §18 config schema: focusaApiBaseUrl should be http://127.0.0.1:8787/v1   §19 env vars: FOCUSA_PI_API_BASE_URL should be http://127.0.0.1:8787/v1  NOTE: §31 already documents this fix. Just execute the find-replace. VERIFY: grep 4777 docs/44-pi-focusa-integration-spec.md returns empty REF: doc 44 §31

title: Pi: communicate context budget to Focusa (prevent overflow)
description: Pi context may have limited headroom. Focusa injects up to 6000 tokens. Without budget feedback, causes overflow and unnecessary compaction. On context event, calculate available headroom from ctx.getContextUsage() and truncate Focusa injection to fit. Per doc 44 §36.7

title: Pi: create focusa-context prompt template
description: Create focusa-context prompt template. CREATE: .pi/prompts/focusa-context.md. Frontmatter: description:Start work with Focusa cognitive context loaded. Content: 1. curl :8787/v1/focus/stack 2. curl :8787/v1/ascc/state 3. Review frame, decisions, constraints 4. Proceed with $ARGUMENTS. VERIFY: /focusa-context loads state. REF: doc 44 §34.2H

title: Pi: define default user workflow (start to finish)
description: Define the default user workflow — what actually happens when Pi starts.  FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts (session_start + before_agent_start hooks)  WORKFLOW:   1. session_start fires:      a. Check Focusa health (GET :8787/v1/health, 2s timeout)      b. If up: register instance (POST /v1/instances/connect)      c. Start Focusa session (POST /v1/session/start)      d. Check for active frame → auto-push if missing (§35.1)      e. Check appendEntry for saved WBM state → restore if found (§33.7)      f. Set session name from frame title (§35.8)      g. Show widget (§37.3)      h. Set status bar: '🧠 Focusa' or '🧠 WBM' or '⚪ No frame'      i. If --wbm flag: enable WBM (§37.5)   2. User works normally — all hooks fire in background   3. No manual steps required except /wbm on (opt-in)  VERIFY: Run pi in project dir. Widget shows frame. Status bar shows Focusa. REF: doc 44 §35.3

title: Pi: extension config validation
description: Pi extension config validation. FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts WHAT: Read .pi/settings.json extensions.focusaPiBridge. Validate: 0<warnPct<compactPct<hardPct<100, cooldownMs≥30000, externalizeThresholdBytes≥2048. Support env var overrides (FOCUSA_PI_*). Reject invalid config on load. REF: doc 44 §18-19

title: Pi CRITICAL: add Focusa behavioral instructions to system prompt
description:
CRITICAL: Add Focusa behavioral instructions to Pi system prompt.  FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts HOOK: before_agent_start FIRES: Once per user prompt (not per LLM call)  IMPLEMENTATION:   return {     systemPrompt: event.systemPrompt + '

' +       '## Focusa Cognitive Governance (Active)
' +       'You are operating within Focusa, a cognitive runtime that preserves focus and decisions.

' +       'RULES:
' +       '- Use the focusa_decide tool when you make a significant decision
' +       '- Use the focusa_constraint tool when you discover a constraint
' +       '- Use the focusa_failure tool when something fails
' +       '- Check the CONSTRAINTS in Focus State before acting — do not violate them
' +       '- The DECISIONS listed below were made earlier — do not contradict without explanation
' +       '- If context was compacted, Focus State below is your source of truth
'   };  NOTE: This fires ONLY in before_agent_start (once per prompt). Live Focus State refresh happens in context event (§33.2, per LLM call). Per §36.6 layering rules: these are complementary, not duplicative.  VERIFY: Check Pi's system prompt includes Focusa instructions. LLM calls focusa_decide tool. DEPENDS: Extension skeleton (focusa-e1w), metacognition tools (focusa-yw9d). REF: doc 44 §35.2, §36.6

title: Pi: signal Context Core when Pi session active/inactive
description: On session_start: wb me --set pi_active=true. On session_shutdown: wb me --set pi_active=false. Context Core needs to know Pi is running for operator state calculations. Per doc 44 §37.9

title: Pipeline fix: historical OpenClaw session batch processor
description: Build historical OpenClaw session batch processor.  OPENCLAW SESSION DIR: /data/wirebot/users/verious/agents/verious/sessions/   2584 session files.  CREATE: /data/wirebot/bin/openclaw-session-extract.sh  LOGIC:   1. List session files sorted by mtime (oldest first)   2. Check watermark: /data/wirebot/scoreboard/openclaw_session_watermark.json   3. For each unprocessed file:      a. Read session file (JSON or JSONL)      b. Extract user + assistant message pairs      c. If >20 messages: chunk into windows of 10 turns      d. For each chunk: POST :8100/v1/memory/extract-conversation         {user_message: chunk_users, assistant_message: chunk_assistants, surface: 'openclaw-session'}      e. Update watermark   4. Rate: 5 sessions per nightly run  VERIFY: After run, wb memory list shows items sourced from openclaw-session. REF: UNIFIED_ORGANISM_SPEC §10A.6 gap 5

title: Pi: extension UI widgets
description: Pi extension UI widgets. FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts WHAT: ctx.ui.setStatus for: focus pressure indicator, degraded-context badge, active frame+thesis snippet, compaction tier badge, /wbm status indicator. REF: doc 44 §10.4

title: Pi: add Pi variant to HarnessType enum
description: Add Pi variant to HarnessType enum in Focusa.  FILE: /home/wirebot/focusa/crates/focusa-core/src/adapters/letta.rs LINE: ~pub enum HarnessType (around line 15-20)  CHANGE:   pub enum HarnessType {       Letta,       ClaudeCode,       CodexCli,       GeminiCli,       Pi,              // ← ADD THIS       Generic(String),   }  ALSO ADD to command() match:   HarnessType::Pi => ""pi"",  ALSO ADD Pi capability declaration:   impl HarnessType {     pub fn capabilities(&self) -> AdapterCapabilities {       match self {         HarnessType::Pi => AdapterCapabilities {           streaming: true,           tool_output_capture: true,           structured_messages: true,           session_management: true,  // Pi has /compact, /fork           slash_commands: true,       // Pi supports /command registration           extension_api: true,        // Pi has full extension hooks         },         ...       }     }   }  BUILD: cd /home/wirebot/focusa && /root/.cargo/bin/cargo build --release TEST: /root/.cargo/bin/cargo test --workspace VERIFY: Grep for HarnessType::Pi in compiled binary. REF: doc 44 §32
notes: CROSS-REF: This changes Focusa Rust code in main repo: crates/focusa-core/src/adapters/letta.rs. Must be built and deployed on VPS (cargo build --release) before Pi extension can use Pi harness type.

title: Pi: spec fidelity audit — code vs canonical Focusa docs
description: Spec fidelity audit: map canonical Focusa docs to code.  METHOD:   1. Read each canonical doc in /home/wirebot/focusa/docs/ (INDEX.md for reading order)   2. For each requirement, find the code that implements it   3. Classify: ✅ implemented+tested / ⚠️ partial/drift / ❌ missing   4. Produce table with: requirement, doc:section, code file:line, status, test reference  PRIORITY AREAS (highest impact on Pi integration):   - Focus Stack invariants (docs/03-focus-stack.md) → crates/focusa-core/src/focus/   - ASCC slot/update/merge (docs/G1-07-ascc.md) → crates/focusa-core/src/ascc.rs   - ECS handle/offload/rehydration (docs/07-reference-store.md) → crates/focusa-core/src/reference/   - Prompt assembly/degradation (docs/G1-detail-11-prompt-assembly.md) → crates/focusa-core/src/expression/   - Focus Gate pressure/surfacing (docs/04-focus-gate.md) → crates/focusa-core/src/gate/   - Write-path auditability → all mutation paths  OUTPUT: Markdown table in /home/wirebot/focusa/docs/SPEC_FIDELITY_AUDIT.md EXIT GATE: No critical drift in Focus Stack, ASCC, ECS, prompt assembly before extension GA. REF: doc 44 §27
notes: CROSS-REF: This audit reads main repo code (crates/focusa-core, crates/focusa-api) against docs. Output goes in main repo docs/SPEC_FIDELITY_AUDIT.md. Blocks pi-extension GA.

title: Pi: register as Focusa instance on session start/shutdown
description: Register Pi as Focusa instance. FILE: focusa-pi-bridge.ts. HOOKS: session_start, session_shutdown. session_start: POST :8787/v1/instances/connect {kind:'Pi'} 2s timeout. session_shutdown: POST :8787/v1/instances/disconnect fire-and-forget. VERIFY: curl :8787/v1/instances/list shows Pi during session. REF: doc 44 §34.2A

title: Pi: create Focusa skill (SKILL.md) for LLM knowledge
description: Create Focusa SKILL.md for Pi. CREATE: ~/.pi/skills/focusa/SKILL.md. Frontmatter: name:focusa, description:Focusa cognitive runtime. Content: How to check status (curl :8787/v1/focus/stack), record decisions (POST /v1/focus/update), read Focus State. Separate from extension — gives LLM knowledge about Focusa. VERIFY: /skill:focusa loads in Pi. REF: doc 44 §34.2G

title: Pi: local decision shadow for offline compaction
description: Maintain local decision shadow for compaction when Focusa down.  FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts STATE: let localDecisions: string[] = [], localConstraints: string[] = [], localFailures: string[] = []  COLLECT (agent_end hook):   For each assistant message in event.messages:     Scan for DECISION:/Decided:/chose to patterns → push to localDecisions     Scan for CONSTRAINT:/FAILURE:/error: patterns → push to localFailures   ALSO: focusa_decide/constraint/failure tool results push to these arrays  USE (session_before_compact hook):   If Focusa ASCC available → use ASCC, clear local arrays (§38.1)   If Focusa down → inject local arrays as customInstructions:     'PRESERVE these: DECISIONS: ...; FAILURES: ...'  TRIM: Clear arrays only after confirmed ASCC write (§38.1). Never clear on fallback. VERIFY: Kill Focusa. Work in Pi. Trigger compaction. Decisions appear in compaction summary. REF: doc 44 §35.4, §38.1

title: Pi: Focus State updates on significant progress
description: When Pi completes a task or makes a decision, POST /v1/focus/update with delta. Even without /wbm, Focusa Focus State should reflect Pi work. Per doc 44 §34.2C

title: Pi: subscribe Focusa SSE for cross-surface event notifications
description: Subscribe Focusa SSE for cross-surface notifications. FILE: focusa-pi-bridge.ts. Connect to :8787/v1/events/stream. On FocusStateUpdated from non-Pi source: ctx.ui.notify('Wirebot: DECISION: X'). Makes Pi real-time participant. Use fetch with ReadableStream (Node.js SSE). Reconnect with backoff on disconnect. VERIFY: Record decision in Wirebot, notification appears in Pi. REF: doc 44 §37.10

title: Pi: keyboard shortcuts Ctrl+Shift+F/W
description: Keyboard shortcuts for Focusa/WBM. FILE: focusa-pi-bridge.ts. pi.registerShortcut('ctrl+shift+f', toggle Focusa status overlay). pi.registerShortcut('ctrl+shift+w', toggle WBM on/off with notification). VERIFY: Ctrl+Shift+W toggles WBM without typing /wbm. REF: doc 44 §37.4

title: Pi: automatic ECS externalization via tool_result
description: Auto-externalize large tool results to ECS. FILE: focusa-pi-bridge.ts. HOOK: tool_result. If text content >8192 bytes: POST :8787/v1/ecs/store {kind:'text', label:toolName+'-output-'+Date.now(), content}. Replace content with handle reference. 3s timeout. On ECS fail: pass through full content. VERIFY: Large bash output externalized, handle reference in context. REF: doc 44 §33.3

title: Pi: detect operator corrections and record as failures + trust
description: On input event, detect 'no that is wrong' / 'undo' / 'I already said' patterns. POST correction as Focus State failure. If /wbm on, feed trust metrics via wb trust set. Per doc 44 §35.7

title: Pi: persist Focusa/WBM state via pi.appendEntry
description: Persist Focusa/WBM state in Pi session via appendEntry.  FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts API: pi.appendEntry('focusa-wbm-state', data)  SAVE (on WBM toggle, periodic, session_shutdown):   pi.appendEntry('focusa-wbm-state', {     sessionId: focusaSessionId,     frameId: activeFrameId,     wbmEnabled: wbmEnabled,     cataloguedDecisions: [...localDecisions],     cataloguedFacts: [...cataloguedFacts],     timestamp: Date.now()   });  RESTORE (on session_start):   for (const entry of ctx.sessionManager.getEntries()) {     if (entry.type === 'custom' && entry.customType === 'focusa-wbm-state') {       // Use LAST entry (most recent state)       restore from entry.data     }   }  ALSO: On resume, reconnect to saved Focusa session (§36.4, focusa-nh1d) VERIFY: /wbm on → exit → /resume → WBM auto-enabled, decisions restored. REF: doc 44 §33.7, §36.4

title: Pi: streaming turn/append via message_update hook
description: Focusa adapter contract requires POST /v1/turn/append for streaming. Use Pi's message_update event to forward text_delta chunks. Required for real-time ASCC extraction and SSE streaming to Agent Audit. Per doc 44 §36.1

title: Pi: Focusa-owned compaction via session_before_compact
description: Focusa-owned compaction via session_before_compact.  FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts HOOK: session_before_compact  IMPLEMENTATION:   1. GET http://127.0.0.1:8787/v1/ascc/state (3s timeout)   2. Build summary from 10 ASCC slots:      intent, current_state, decisions[], constraints[], failures[],      next_steps[], open_questions[], recent_results[], notes[], artifacts[]   3. Return { compaction: { summary, firstKeptEntryId: event.preparation.firstKeptEntryId, tokensBefore: event.preparation.tokensBefore } }   4. On success: clear localDecisions/localConstraints/localFailures (§38.1)   5. On Focusa timeout: fall back to local shadow customInstructions (§35.4)   6. On both fail: return undefined (Pi's default compaction)  VERIFY: Trigger compaction in long Pi session. Compaction summary contains structured ASCC, not generic text. DEPENDS: Focusa daemon running, ASCC state endpoint working. REF: doc 44 §33.1, §35.4, §38.1

title: Pi: enforce session scoping — Pi sees only Pi cognition
description: Enforce session scoping — Pi sees only Pi cognition.  FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts  RULE: All Focusa API calls from the extension must include session_id parameter.   When Pi starts, it should call POST /v1/session/start to get a Pi-specific session_id.   All subsequent GET calls include ?session_id=$PI_SESSION_ID    WHAT PI MUST NOT ACCESS:   - /v1/autonomy (Wirebot's ARI, not Pi's — unless session-scoped)   - Scoreboard API (:8100) — Wirebot only   - Context Core (:7400) — Wirebot only (UNLESS /wbm is active)   - Mem0 (:8200) — Wirebot only (UNLESS /wbm is active)   - Trust metrics — Wirebot only  WHAT PI CAN ACCESS (own session):   - /v1/focus/stack?session_id=$ID — Pi's focus frames   - /v1/focus/state?session_id=$ID — Pi's focus state   - /v1/events/recent?session_id=$ID — Pi's events   - /v1/ecs/handles?session_id=$ID — Pi's artifacts   - /v1/constitution/active — shared (read-only)   - /v1/gate/scores?session_id=$ID — Pi's gate candidates  EXCEPTION: When /wbm is ON, Pi can also access Wirebot context (per doc 44 §29).  VERIFY: Without /wbm, Pi cannot see Wirebot's focus stack or memories. REF: doc 44 §28

title: Pi: long-session stress test (200+ turns)
description: Pi long-session stress test (200+ turns). PROCEDURE: Run Pi through focusa wrap -- pi. Execute 200+ turns of real coding work. Monitor: context usage, compaction triggers, ECS offload, degradation order, token growth curve. VERIFY: Session survives 200 turns without overflow. Compaction fires at expected thresholds. REF: doc 44 §15 test 1

title: Pi CRITICAL: auto-push Focus Frame on session start
description: CRITICAL: Auto-push Focus Frame when Pi session starts.  FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts (or focusa-pi-bridge/index.ts) HOOK: session_start  IMPLEMENTATION:   1. GET http://127.0.0.1:8787/v1/focus/stack (2s timeout)   2. If stack.active_frame_id exists → use existing frame, skip push   3. If no active frame:      a. Derive project name: ctx.cwd.split('/').pop()      b. Check for beads: pi.exec('bd', ['ready'], {timeout:3000})         If result: use first bead ID as beads_issue_id         If no result: use 'pi-session-' + Date.now()      c. POST http://127.0.0.1:8787/v1/focus/push         Body: {title: 'Pi: $projectName', goal: 'Work on $projectName', beads_issue_id: $beadsId, constraints: [], tags: ['pi', projectName]}   4. On failure: log warning, continue without frame (degraded mode)  VERIFY: Start Pi in a project directory. curl :8787/v1/focus/stack shows new frame. DEPENDS: Focusa daemon running. Extension skeleton exists (focusa-e1w). REF: doc 44 §35.1

title: Pipeline fix: diary extraction through LLM not raw text push
description: Fix diary extraction to use LLM instead of raw text push.  FILE: /data/wirebot/bin/wiki-enrich-nightly.sh FUNCTION: queue_and_approve_memory() around line 119  CURRENT (broken):   def queue_and_approve_memory(text, source_file, source_context):     body = {memory_text: text, ...}  # Raw text, no LLM     POST /v1/memory/queue  # Create     POST /v1/memory/queue/{id}/approve  # AUTO-APPROVE (skips WINS UI!)  REQUIRED:   Replace with:     1. POST :8100/v1/memory/extract-vault with diary wiki page path        This calls ExtractMemoriesFromDocument which uses LLM     2. Extracted memories go through QueueMemoryForApproval (status: pending)     3. Operator reviews in WINS portal (NOT auto-approved)        OR: Change the function to call the scoreboard's LLM extraction:     POST :8100/v1/memory/extract-conversation        {user_message: diary_content, assistant_message: '', surface: 'nightly_diary'}  REMOVE: The auto-approve POST to /v1/memory/queue/{id}/approve  VERIFY: After nightly run, wb memory list shows pending (not approved) diary extractions. REF: UNIFIED_ORGANISM_SPEC §10A.6 gap 1

title: Pi: sync session name from Focus Frame title
description: Sync Pi session name from Focus Frame title. FILE: focusa-pi-bridge.ts. After frame push or on session_start: fetch frame title from :8787/v1/focus/stack, call pi.setSessionName('🧠 ' + frame.title). VERIFY: /resume shows meaningful frame names. REF: doc 44 §35.8

title: Pi: submit intuition signals for pattern detection
description: Pi detects repeated compilation errors → POST /v1/intuition/submit. Session >2h → long-running signal. Many edits to same file → churn signal. Per doc 44 §34.2D

title: Pi CRITICAL: define injection layering rules (prevent double-injection)
description: CRITICAL: Define and enforce injection layering rules.  FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts AFFECTS: All injection hooks  RULES (authoritative):   before_agent_start → behavioral instructions ONLY (§35.2). Fires once per prompt. ALWAYS ACTIVE.   context → live Focus State refresh (§33.2). Fires per LLM call. ALWAYS ACTIVE.   before_provider_request → DISABLED. Mutually exclusive with context event. §33.5 superseded.   session_before_compact → ASCC compaction summary (§33.1). Fires on compact only.  IMPLEMENTATION: In the extension, do NOT register a before_provider_request handler.   Comment: // §36.6: before_provider_request DISABLED — using context event instead  VERIFY: Set breakpoint or log in all 4 hooks. On a single turn, verify:   before_agent_start fires 1x (instructions)   context fires 1x per LLM call (state)   before_provider_request does NOT fire   Focus State appears ONCE in the assembled prompt, not 2-3x REF: doc 44 §36.6

title: Pi: feed modified files from compaction to Focusa artifacts
description: Pi tracks readFiles[] and modifiedFiles[] in compaction details. On session_compact, POST to /v1/focus/update with artifacts[] from modifiedFiles. Per doc 44 §35.6

title: Pi: validate focusa wrap -- pi end-to-end
description: Validate focusa wrap -- pi end-to-end.  PROCEDURE:   1. cd /home/wirebot/focusa && ./target/release/focusa-daemon (ensure running)   2. focusa wrap -- pi (start Pi through Focusa wrapper)   3. In wrapped Pi session:      a. Send a prompt — verify Focusa records TurnStarted event      b. Check curl :8787/v1/events/recent — verify turn events appear      c. Push a focus frame: curl -X POST :8787/v1/focus/push      d. Verify prompt assembly includes focus context      e. Complete a multi-turn task — verify ASCC delta extraction      f. Check ECS: if large tool output, verify handle created   4. Exit Pi — verify session_closed event  KNOWN ISSUES (from doc 44 §26):   - Turn telemetry may show prompt_tokens=null   - Focus Gate candidates often empty   - CLT payloads may contain terminal escape sequences   - Large directives may dilute salience  VERIFY: All 6 checks pass. Events in Focusa log match Pi activity. DEPENDS: Focusa daemon running, Pi installed REF: doc 44 §14 Phase 1, §26

title: Pi: metacognitive UX indicators
description: Pi metacognitive UX indicators. FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts WHAT: Subscribe to Focusa SSE (:8787/v1/events/stream). Show: 🧠 thinking when workers run, 📝 extracted N decisions when extraction completes, 🎯 thesis updated when thesis refines, ⚠️ quality flag when evaluation finds issues. Informational only. REF: doc 44 §30

title: Pi: Focusa outage test (passthrough correctness)
description: Focusa outage test. PROCEDURE: 1. Start Pi through Focusa. 2. Kill Focusa daemon mid-session. 3. Verify Pi continues (passthrough). 4. Restart Focusa. 5. Verify reconnection + state resync. VERIFY: Pi never crashes. Focusa reconnects. State recovered. REF: doc 44 §15 test 4

title: Pi: extension compaction tier logic
description: Pi extension compaction tier logic. FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts WHAT: on turn_end, compute usagePct = currentTokens/contextWindow*100. Apply: warn≥50%, compact≥70%, hard≥85%. Submit Focusa command for compaction. Cooldown 180s, max 8/hour, min 3 turns between. REF: doc 44 §20

title: Pi: build focusa-pi-bridge.ts extension skeleton
description: Create focusa-pi-bridge.ts Pi extension skeleton.  CREATE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts  SKELETON:   import type { ExtensionAPI } from '@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent';   import { Type } from '@sinclair/typebox';      export default function (pi: ExtensionAPI) {     // State     let wbmEnabled = false;     let wbmContext: string | null = null;     let cataloguedDecisions: string[] = [];     let cataloguedFacts: string[] = [];          // Register commands (doc 44 §10.3)     pi.registerCommand('focusa-status', { description: 'Focusa daemon status', handler: async (args, ctx) => {       const res = await fetch('http://127.0.0.1:8787/v1/health', {signal: AbortSignal.timeout(2000)});       ctx.ui.notify(JSON.stringify(await res.json()), 'info');     }});     pi.registerCommand('focusa-stack', { ... });     pi.registerCommand('focusa-pin', { ... });     pi.registerCommand('focusa-suppress', { ... });     pi.registerCommand('focusa-checkpoint', { ... });     pi.registerCommand('focusa-rehydrate', { ... });     pi.registerCommand('focusa-gate-explain', { ... });     pi.registerCommand('wbm', { ... });  // From focusa-wzk bead          // Hooks     pi.on('before_agent_start', async (event, ctx) => { ... });  // Context injection     pi.on('agent_end', async (event, ctx) => { ... });  // Work cataloguing     pi.on('turn_start', async (event, ctx) => { ... });  // Status indicator   }  API CALLS: All to http://127.0.0.1:8787/v1/* with 2s timeout + AbortSignal FALLBACK: If Focusa unavailable, skip all hooks silently (fail-safe per doc 44 §11)  VERIFY: pi loads extension without errors. /focusa-status returns health JSON. DEPENDS: Focusa daemon running REF: doc 44 §10

title: Pipeline fix: full-conversation extraction (not just last message)
description: Fix conversation extraction to send full conversation, not just last message.  FILE: /home/wirebot/wirebot-core/plugins/wirebot-memory-bridge/index.ts LINES: 1587-1604  CURRENT (broken):   Line 1587: const lastUser = conversation.filter((m) => m.role === 'user').pop();   Line 1588: const lastAssistant = conversation.filter((m) => m.role === 'assistant').pop();   Lines 1596-1604: Sends only lastUser.content + lastAssistant.content to extract-conversation  REQUIRED CHANGE:   1. Collect ALL user+assistant messages from conversation array (not just .pop())   2. If >20 messages, chunk into windows of 10-20 turns   3. For each chunk, POST to :8100/v1/memory/extract-conversation   4. Remove the 2-minute rate limit at lines ~848-852 in memory_extract.go       (convoExtractMu / lastConvoExtractTime check) for session-end extraction      OR: create a new endpoint /v1/memory/extract-session that accepts full conversation array  ALSO FIX: Lines 1458-1483 (the Mem0 direct extraction path) — same issue, only sends last message.  VERIFY: After a 30-message Wirebot conversation, wb memory list shows memories extracted from messages 1-28, not just 29-30. REF: UNIFIED_ORGANISM_SPEC §10A.6 gap 2

title: Pi: flush Focusa state on session_before_switch
description: Flush Focusa state before Pi session switch. FILE: focusa-pi-bridge.ts. HOOK: session_before_switch. On /new or /resume: 1. flushWorkMeta() 2. persistFocusaState() 3. POST :8787/v1/session/close {reason:'pi_session_switch'} 2s timeout. VERIFY: /new previous session fully flushed. REF: doc 44 §37.7

title: Pi: cross-harness continuity test (Pi → Claude → Pi)
description: Cross-harness continuity test (Pi → Claude → Pi). PROCEDURE: 1. Start Pi session through Focusa, push frame, make decisions. 2. Exit Pi. 3. Start Claude Code through Focusa. Verify Focus State preserved. 4. Return to Pi. Verify state still intact. VERIFY: Focus State decisions survive harness switch. REF: doc 44 §15 test 5

title: Pi: HTTP fallback for wb CLI when running on Mac
description: Every wb CLI call (wiki search, memory inject, etc) must have direct HTTP fallback to Wiki.js GraphQL (:7325), scoreboard (:8100), Context Core (:7400). Config: focusaPiBridge.scoreboardUrl, wikiUrl, contextCoreUrl for remote machine setup. Extension works on any machine with network access. Per doc 44 §38.2

title: Pipeline fix: build Pi session JSONL parser for extraction
description: Build Pi session JSONL parser for historical backfill.  PI SESSION FORMAT: ~/.pi/agent/sessions/<dir>/*.jsonl   Each line is a JSON object with: type, role, content, toolCalls, etc.   111 files, 1.7GB total.  CREATE: /data/wirebot/bin/pi-session-extract.sh  LOGIC:   1. List session files sorted by mtime (oldest first)   2. Check watermark: /data/wirebot/scoreboard/pi_session_watermark.json   3. For each unprocessed file:      a. Read .jsonl, parse each line      b. Filter: keep role=assistant messages with content.length > 200      c. Pair with preceding role=user messages      d. Chunk into windows of 15 turns      e. For each chunk: POST :8100/v1/memory/extract-conversation         {user_message: chunk_users, assistant_message: chunk_assistants, surface: 'pi-session'}      f. Update watermark   4. Rate: 2 sessions per nightly run, 2s between chunks    NOTE: Live Pi extraction handled by /wbm extension (focusa-6dm). This is ONE-TIME backfill only. After 111 files processed (~56 nights), retire this script. VERIFY: After run, wb memory list shows items sourced from pi-session. REF: UNIFIED_ORGANISM_SPEC §10A.4, §10A.6 gap 4

title: Pi: custom message renderer for Focusa context blocks
description: Custom renderer for Focusa context messages. FILE: focusa-pi-bridge.ts. API: pi.registerMessageRenderer('focusa-state', ...). Collapsed: dim icon + 'Focusa Context'. Expanded: full state. Prevents injected context from looking like user messages. Import Text from @mariozechner/pi-tui. VERIFY: Focusa blocks show collapsed by default. REF: doc 44 §37.6

title: Pi: disable focusa tools when daemon down via setActiveTools
description: Disable focusa tools when daemon down. FILE: focusa-pi-bridge.ts. Check :8787/v1/health on session_start and every 60s. If down: pi.setActiveTools(current.filter(n => !n.startsWith('focusa_'))). If back: re-add focusa_decide/constraint/failure. ctx.ui.notify on state change. VERIFY: Kill Focusa, tools disappear. Restart, tools reappear. REF: doc 44 §38.3

title: Pipeline fix: change EXTRACTION_MODEL from Kimi to MiniMax M2.7
description: Change extraction model from expensive Kimi to cheap MiniMax M2.7.  FILE: /data/wirebot/scoreboard/scoreboard.env ADD LINE: EXTRACTION_MODEL=minimax/MiniMax-M2.7  CODE THAT READS IT: /home/wirebot/wirebot-core/cmd/scoreboard/memory_extract.go line 629:   '""model"": envOr(""EXTRACTION_MODEL"", ""kimi-coding/k2p5"")'  The envOr fallback is kimi-coding/k2p5. Setting the env var overrides to MiniMax. NOTE: The extraction calls go through OpenClaw gateway (/v1/chat/completions).  Gateway must have MiniMax provider configured. Check /data/wirebot/users/verious/openclaw.json for minimax provider.  RESTART: systemctl restart wirebot-scoreboard VERIFY: Trigger a vault extraction, check scoreboard logs for model=MiniMax-M2.7 REF: UNIFIED_ORGANISM_SPEC §10A.6

title: Pi: --wbm and --focusa CLI flags
description: Register --wbm and --focusa CLI flags. FILE: focusa-pi-bridge.ts. pi.registerFlag('wbm', {type:'boolean', default:false, description:'Start with Wirebot Mode'}). pi.registerFlag('focusa', {type:'boolean', default:true, description:'Enable Focusa'}). On session_start: if(pi.getFlag('--wbm')) wbmEnabled=true. if(!pi.getFlag('--focusa')) focusaEnabled=false. VERIFY: pi --wbm starts with WBM on. REF: doc 44 §37.5

title: Pi: per-LLM-call context injection via context event
description:
Inject live Focus State per LLM call via context event.  FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts HOOK: context (fires before EVERY LLM call, not just first)  IMPLEMENTATION:   1. Check focusaEnabled flag   2. Calculate available headroom: ctx.getContextUsage() (§36.7)      maxFocusaTokens = min(max(headroom * 0.2, 200), 2000)   3. GET http://127.0.0.1:8787/v1/ascc/state (cached 5s TTL, 2s timeout)   4. Truncate state to maxFocusaTokens   5. Build message: {role: 'user', content: [{type: 'text', text: '[FOCUSA STATE]
Frame: ...
Decisions: ...
Constraints: ...'}], timestamp: Date.now()}   6. Return { messages: [focusaMsg, ...event.messages] }   7. On Focusa timeout: skip injection, use cached if available  NOTE: This is the SECONDARY injection (live state refresh). PRIMARY is before_agent_start (behavioral instructions, §35.2). Per §36.6 layering rules.  VERIFY: Multi-tool turn (5 tool calls). Each LLM call includes updated Focus State. DEPENDS: Extension skeleton, Focusa running. REF: doc 44 §33.2, §36.6, §36.7

title: Pi: track model changes for Focusa telemetry
description: On model_select event, POST /v1/focus/update with model change note. Different models have different costs, Focusa telemetry needs to know. Per doc 44 §37.8

title: Pi: Focusa outage passthrough + auto-reconnect
description: Implement Focusa outage passthrough in Pi extension.  FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts  IN EVERY Focusa API call, wrap with:   try {     const res = await fetch(url, { signal: AbortSignal.timeout(2000) });     if (!res.ok) throw new Error(res.status);     return await res.json();   } catch (e) {     ctx.ui.notify('Focusa unavailable — passthrough mode', 'warn');     return null;  // caller skips Focusa features   }  AUTO-RECONNECT:   let focusaDown = false;   let reconnectAttempt = 0;      On failure: focusaDown = true, reconnectAttempt++   Every 30s while focusaDown: try GET :8787/v1/health     If success: focusaDown = false, reconnectAttempt = 0, ctx.ui.notify('Focusa reconnected')   Backoff: Math.min(30 * 2^reconnectAttempt, 300) seconds  SOFT RESYNC on reconnect:   Fetch /v1/focus/stack, /v1/constitution/active to restore cached state  VERIFY: Kill Focusa, Pi continues working. Restart Focusa, Pi reconnects within 60s. REF: doc 44 §11

title: Pi: error signals to Focus Gate (tool errors + model errors)
description: tool_result with isError=true → POST /v1/focus-gate/ingest-signal signal_type:tool_error. message_end with stopReason=error → signal_type:model_error. Repeated errors trigger Intuition Engine patterns. Per doc 44 §36.2

title: Pi: /wbm cataloguing — outbound work meta extraction
description: Implement /wbm outbound work meta extraction.  FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts  ON agent_end (when wbmEnabled):   1. Collect event.messages (full conversation from this prompt cycle)   2. Filter: keep user + assistant messages with >50 chars   3. If >20 messages: chunk into windows of 15 turns   4. For each chunk: call MiniMax M2.7 for extraction      POST http://127.0.0.1:8100/v1/memory/extract-conversation        { user_message: chunk_user_text, assistant_message: chunk_assistant_text, surface: 'pi' }      This routes through existing QueueMemoryForApproval → WINS portal   5. Also extract decisions/failures directly:      Call MiniMax M2.7 (via OpenClaw gateway :18789/v1/chat/completions)        model: minimax/MiniMax-M2.7, max_tokens: 500, timeout: 2s        prompt: 'Extract decisions, facts, failures, learnings from this exchange. Return JSON.'   6. Route decisions to Focusa: POST :8787/v1/focus/state/update {decisions: [...], failures: [...]}    ALL items tagged: source_type: 'pi_session', source:pi, surface:pi SHIPS: NOT manually catalogued — git discovery handles this.  FALLBACK: LLM extraction fails → skip cataloguing for this turn. Log warning. VERIFY: After Pi session with /wbm on, wb memory list shows pending items from pi. WINS portal shows pi-sourced memories. DEPENDS: focusa-e1w (skeleton), focusa-wzk (/wbm command), focusa-621 (extraction model) REF: doc 44 §29, UNIFIED_ORGANISM_SPEC §9.9

title: Pi historical extraction: process compaction summaries FIRST
description: Pi .jsonl sessions contain CompactionEntry with pre-digested LLM summaries. Historical extraction should process these FIRST (high signal, low cost) before raw messages. Novel insight from session.md format analysis. Per doc 44 §34.3

title: Pi: add /focusa-explain-decision and /focusa-lineage commands
description: /focusa-explain-decision → GET /v1/state/explain. /focusa-lineage → GET /v1/lineage/head + /v1/lineage/path. Untapped provenance/explanation routes. Per doc 44 §34.2E-F

title: Pi: trim local decision shadow after successful ASCC write
description: Trim local decision shadow after ASCC write. FILE: focusa-pi-bridge.ts. In session_before_compact handler: after confirmed ASCC write (§33.1 returns compaction), clear localDecisions/localConstraints/localFailures. NEVER clear when falling back to local shadow (Focusa down). VERIFY: After compaction with Focusa up, local arrays empty. REF: doc 44 §38.1

title: Pi: user input signals to Focus Gate
description: On agent_start, POST /v1/focus-gate/ingest-signal signal_type:user_input. Helps Intuition Engine track activity cadence for inactivity detection. Adapter contract requires this. Per doc 44 §36.3

title: Pi: extension continuous pruning loop
description: Pi extension continuous pruning loop. FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts WHAT: Per tool result: if output>8KB, externalize to ECS via Focusa API. Every 5 turns: request micro-compact via Focusa command. Smooth token growth curve. REF: doc 44 §21

title: Pi: turn-level tracking (turn/start + turn/complete)
description: On turn_start: POST /v1/turn/start. On turn_end: POST /v1/turn/complete with assistant output. CRITICAL: without this Focusa cannot score autonomy, run workers, build ASCC deltas, or track CLT lineage from Pi sessions. Per doc 44 §34.2B

title: Pi: tool usage tracking for autonomy via tool_call
description: Use tool_call hook to fire-and-forget tool usage signals to Focusa Intuition Engine. Enables pattern detection: repeated failures, tool-hopping (RABIT), excessive writes. Per doc 44 §33.4

title: Pi: fix route reference /v1/gate/ingest-signal → /v1/focus-gate/ingest-signal
description: Fix route: /v1/gate/ingest-signal → /v1/focus-gate/ingest-signal.  FILES: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts (all signal ingestion calls) ALSO: doc 44 §33.4 text reference  SEARCH in extension code: /v1/gate/ingest-signal REPLACE with: /v1/focus-gate/ingest-signal  VERIFIED: grep in crates/focusa-api/src/routes/gate.rs confirms:   POST /v1/focus-gate/ingest-signal is the actual route. REF: doc 44 §34.1
notes: CROSS-REF: Route fix is in doc 44 text only. No Focusa Rust code change needed — the correct route /v1/focus-gate/ingest-signal already exists in crates/focusa-api/src/routes/gate.rs.

title: Pipeline fix: upgrade wiki sink from bullet append to knowledge pages
description: Upgrade wiki sink from bullet append to knowledge pages.  FILE: /home/wirebot/wirebot-core/cmd/scoreboard/memory.go FUNCTION: wikiAppendFact() around line after executeMemorySink  CURRENT (shallow):   Appends '- [date source_type] fact' to a single 'Recent Facts' page.   No decision pages. No wiki links. No schema. No MOC connection.  REQUIRED:   Replace wikiAppendFact with wikiDeliverMemory that:   1. Classify memory type: decision / skill / fact / failure   2. IF DECISION:      wb wiki create --title 'Decision: $TITLE' --path ops/decisions/$DATE-$SLUG \        --tags decision,$SOURCE --desc 'Auto-extracted from $SOURCE'      Content: rationale, alternatives if available, project link   3. IF SKILL/PATTERN:      wb wiki search existing skill page → update, or create new      Ensure MOC link   4. IF FACT:      Append to relevant project/concept page (not generic 'Recent Facts')      wb wiki search for matching page → append as bullet under ## Facts   5. ALL: schema-valid metadata, provenance notes, ≥1 wiki link  REBUILD: cd /home/wirebot/wirebot-core && go build -o cmd/scoreboard/wirebot-scoreboard ./cmd/scoreboard/ RESTART: systemctl restart wirebot-scoreboard VERIFY: Approve a memory in WINS portal. Check wiki for decision page (not just bullet). REF: UNIFIED_ORGANISM_SPEC §10A.6 gap 6

title: Pi: register focusa_decide/constraint/failure TOOLS for LLM
description: Register focusa_decide/constraint/failure as Pi tools.  FILE: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/focusa-pi-bridge.ts API: pi.registerTool()  3 TOOLS:   1. focusa_decide: params {decision: string, rationale: string, alternatives?: string[]}      Handler: POST :8787/v1/focus/update {decisions: ['$decision (because: $rationale)']}      If wbm: also pi.exec('wb', ['memory', 'inject', 'DECISION: $decision...'])      Push to localDecisions array for local shadow      Return: '✅ Decision recorded: $decision'      promptGuidelines: ['When you make a significant choice, call focusa_decide.']    2. focusa_constraint: params {constraint: string, reason?: string}      Handler: POST :8787/v1/focus/update {constraints: ['$constraint']}      Push to localConstraints      Return: '⚠️ Constraint recorded: $constraint'      promptGuidelines: ['When you discover a constraint, call focusa_constraint.']    3. focusa_failure: params {failure: string, impact?: string}      Handler: POST :8787/v1/focus/update {failures: ['$failure']}      Push to localFailures      Return: '❌ Failure recorded: $failure'      promptGuidelines: ['When something fails, call focusa_failure.']  All tools: 2s timeout on Focusa call. On failure: still push to local shadow + return success. Use Type.Object from @sinclair/typebox for params. Use promptSnippet: 'Record decisions, constraints, and failures for cognitive tracking'  VERIFY: Ask LLM to make a decision. It calls focusa_decide. curl :8787/v1/focus/stack shows decision. DEPENDS: Extension skeleton (focusa-e1w). Focusa daemon running. REF: doc 44 §37.2

title: Pi: handle /fork and /tree — snapshot Focus State per branch
description: On session_fork: snapshot Focus State at fork point. On session_tree: restore Focus State for target branch. Requires Focusa core support for per-CLT-node state snapshots. Both extension + Rust changes. Per doc 44 §36.5
notes: CROSS-REF: Requires Focusa core change in main repo: per-CLT-node Focus State snapshots. Extension does the fork/tree handling, but Focusa daemon must support snapshot/restore. Both repos involved.

title: Pi: Focusa-guided compaction instructions
description: Softer compaction: inject Focus State as customInstructions. FILE: focusa-pi-bridge.ts. HOOK: session_before_compact. FALLBACK when §33.1 ASCC returns empty but Focusa reachable. return {customInstructions:'PRESERVE: Intent... Decisions... Constraints... Failures...'}. Primary is focusa-qt94. VERIFY: compaction summary mentions decisions. REF: doc 44 §33.10
